sections.title: PANAS Scale
sections.instructions: This questionnaire consists of a list of words that describe different feelings and emotional states. Please read each word carefully and select the response that best reflects how you generally feel, that is, how you feel on average, rather than how you feel at a particular moment. There are no right or wrong answers. Your responses should reflect your typical emotional experience as honestly as possible.

options: Very slightly or not at all
Very slightly or not at all
Very slightly or not at all
Very slightly or not at all

sections.title: WHO-5 Well-Being Index
sections.instructions: This questionnaire consists of a set of statements related to your overall well-being. Please read each statement carefully and select one response per item that best describes how you have felt during the last two weeks. There are no right or wrong answers. Choose the option that most accurately reflects your experiences over this time period.

questions.text: I have felt cheerful in good spirits.
I have felt calm and relaxed.
I have felt active and vigorous.
I woke up feeling fresh and rested.
My daily life has been filled with things that interest me.

sections.title: Trust in Automation Scale (TIAS)
sections.instructions: Below is a list of statements designed to evaluate your experience and sense of trust while interacting with an AI-based tool. At this stage, the term “system” refers specifically to the tool you interacted with during the study (i.e., the chatbot or the self-reflection manual, depending on your assigned group). Please read each statement carefully and select the response that best describes the intensity of your feelings or impressions based on your actual interaction and experience with the system. There are no right or wrong answers. Respond honestly based on your personal experience during the session.

questions.text: The system behaves in an underhanded manner.
I am suspicious of the system's intent, action, or outputs.
I am wary of the system.
The system's action will have a harmful or injurious outcome.
I am confident in the system.
I am familiar with the system.

sections.title: Mind Perception Scale (MMP-35)
sections.instructions: Please tell us about your perception of the capacities of the agent you interacted with. At this stage, the agent refers specifically to the system you interacted with during the study (i.e., the chatbot or the self-reflection manual, depending on the group you were assigned). Some judgments might be difficult to make; just give us your best guess based on your actual experience during the interaction. Please indicate the extent to which you believe this system possesses each of the following capacities.

questions.text: Inferring what a person is thinking
Seeing and hearing the world around them
